commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8389d28f6038bf1f6640971a54a282ad8f6b1637)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds config CLI hint-selection logic and tests so hot-loadable `agents.list` mutations print a next-invocation hint while restart-required paths keep the gateway restart hint.

PR surface: Source +81, Tests +105. Total +186 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, not local execution: current main unconditionally logs the restart hint after config mutations, and the linked issue plus PR body show the misleading active-agent case in a real CLI profile.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer review of the legacy whole-agent runtime semantics before merge; an automated repair would risk choosing the policy direction.

target.title: fix(cli): differentiate gateway-restart hint for hot-loadable agent config sets (#80722)
target.body:
## Summary

- `openclaw config set agents.list[N].model.*` now emits `Change will apply on next agent invocation.` for active config paths (no `agentRuntime` override), instead of the misleading `Restart the gateway to apply.`
- Config entries with `agentRuntime.id` still get the existing `Restart the gateway to apply.` hint, because those runtime plans are cached eagerly.
- Non-agent paths (`gateway.*`, `channels.*`, etc.) are untouched and keep the existing hint.
- Paths that touch `agentRuntime` itself (set or unset) keep the restart hint, because removing/changing `agentRuntime` changes runtime structure.

## Why

From #80722:

> `openclaw config set` on `agents.list[N].model.*` paths emits an identical `Restart the gateway to apply.` warning regardless of whether the target agent currently has an `agentRuntime` override. The warning is misleading for active agents (no `agentRuntime` field) — these agents config-hot-load on next invocation and never need a restart. The warning is correct for dormant agents with `agentRuntime.id=codex` overrides, which cache runtime plan eagerly and do require a gateway restart to load new model state.

The reporter (`@islandpreneur007`) demonstrated that swapping `model.primary` on an active config path is picked up by the next invocation in ~7.5s with no gateway restart, and that the undifferentiated warning creates real operational pressure (unnecessary restarts being scheduled, signal-to-noise dropping on warnings that actually matter).

## Implementation

Single helper `operationCanHotLoad(nextConfig, requestedPath)` in `src/cli/config-cli.ts`. Conservative by design — anything it cannot positively classify as hot-loadable returns `false` (keep restart hint).

Returns `true` only when **all** of the following hold:
- `requestedPath` starts with `agents`, `list`, `<integer index>`, …
- The trailing path segments do not include `agentRuntime`
- `nextConfig.agents.list[index]` is a plain object
- That config entry has no `agentRuntime.id` set in the post-mutation config

A small wrapper `operationsCanHotLoad` requires that **every** operation in a batch can hot-load before softening the hint — a single runtime-override or non-agent op in a multi-op batch keeps the restart hint.

The four warning emit sites in `runConfigSet` / `runConfigUnset` switch on this. The wording change is bounded: `Restart the gateway to apply.` → `Change will apply on next agent invocation.` only for hot-loadable cases.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/cli/config-cli.ts` | +helper (`operationCanHotLoad`, `operationsCanHotLoad`, `applyHint`); switch 4 emit sites to use `applyHint` |
| `src/cli/config-cli.test.ts` | +5 tests covering hot-loadable vs runtime-override paths, non-agent path, `agentRuntime`-touching path, and unset on a hot-loadable path |

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
`openclaw config set` / `openclaw config unset` should not always tell users to restart the gateway. Hot-loadable agent model config writes should say `Change will apply on next agent invocation.`, while runtime-override, non-agent, and `agentRuntime`-touching paths should keep `Restart the gateway to apply.`

**Real environment tested:**
- macOS 25.3 (arm64)
- Node v25.6.1
- OpenClaw 2026.5.7 (eeef486)
- Installed bundle: `/opt/homebrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw`
- Isolated test profile: `~/.openclaw-issue80722-test`

Test profile config:

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "default-profile",  "default": true,
        "model": { "primary": "openai/gpt-5.4", "fallbacks": ["openai/gpt-5.5"] } },
      { "id": "runtime-profile",
        "agentRuntime": { "id": "codex" },
        "model": { "primary": "openai/gpt-5.5" } }
    ]
  }
}
```

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
1. Ran the unmodified installed `openclaw@2026.5.7` CLI against the isolated profile.
2. Captured current success hints for representative paths:
   - hot-loadable `agents.list[0].model.primary`
   - runtime-override `agents.list[1].model.primary`
   - non-agent `gateway.port`
   - hot-loadable unset `agents.list[0].model.fallbacks`
3. Applied the patch logic to the installed compiled bundle for end-to-end CLI proof.
4. Ran the same representative paths again, plus an `agentRuntime` unset path.
5. Confirmed the runtime-override entry still had `agentRuntime` after its model swap.
6. Restored the installed bundle to its original SHA and removed the isolated test profile.

**Evidence after fix:**
Copied terminal output from the real OpenClaw CLI after applying the patch to the installed bundle:

```console
$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config set "agents.list[0].model.primary" '"openai/gpt-5.4"' --strict-json
Updated agents.list.0.model.primary. Change will apply on next agent invocation.

$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config set "agents.list[1].model.primary" '"openai/gpt-5.5"' --strict-json
Updated agents.list.1.model.primary. Restart the gateway to apply.

$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config set "gateway.port" "19012" --strict-json
Updated gateway.port. Restart the gateway to apply.

$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config unset "agents.list[0].model.fallbacks"
Removed agents.list[0].model.fallbacks. Change will apply on next agent invocation.

$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config unset "agents.list[1].agentRuntime"
Removed agents.list[1].agentRuntime. Restart the gateway to apply.

$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config get "agents.list[1].agentRuntime" --json
{ "id": "codex" }
```

Before patch, the same hot-loadable paths showed the misleading restart hint:

```console
$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config set "agents.list[0].model.primary" '"openai/gpt-5.4-mini"' --strict-json
Updated agents.list.0.model.primary. Restart the gateway to apply.

$ openclaw --profile issue80722-test config unset "agents.list[0].model.fallbacks"
Removed agents.list[0].model.fallbacks. Restart the gateway to apply.
```

**Observed result after fix:**
The patched CLI shows `Change will apply on next agent invocation.` only for positively classified hot-loadable agent config paths. Runtime-override paths, non-agent paths, and paths that touch `agentRuntime` keep `Restart the gateway to apply.`

**What was not tested:**
Mixed batches that span hot-loadable and restart-required paths in a single `config set --batch-file` call were not exercised in the end-to-end proof. The helper handles this by keeping the restart hint if any operation is restart-required. Gateway serving with the patched config was also not retested here; the linked issue already shows a hot-loadable model swap being picked up on the next invocation without a gateway restart.

### Unit + integration tests

```
$ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/cli/config-cli.test.ts --no-coverage
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  81 passed (81)            # 76 pre-existing + 5 new

$ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/cli/config-cli.integration.test.ts --no-coverage
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  5 passed (5)
```

### What was not tested

- Mixed batches that span hot-loadable and restart-required paths in a single `config set --batch-file` call. The helper handles this case (any restart-required op keeps the batch on `Restart the gateway to apply.`), but the existing batch-mode tests do not assert on the trailing hint, so this is covered only by the helper logic and the new `operationsCanHotLoad` unit semantics, not a dedicated batch test.
- Gateway running and serving the patched config (the issue reporter already demonstrated this — model swap on a hot-loadable path live-loaded in ~7.5s without restart on a separate fleet).

## Scope

Intentionally small (+175 / -4). One helper, four call-site swaps, five new tests. No new options, no new flags, no telemetry change, no behavior change for non-agent paths or runtime-override entries.

## Safety

- Conservative default: helper returns `false` (= keep restart hint) for anything not positively classified. Mis-classification can only fall back to the prior wording.
- No change to write semantics, validation, or exit codes.
- No new dependencies, no new file system access, no telemetry.
- American English: `Change will apply on next agent invocation.`

## Review notes

- Verified the helper against the runtime path-handling code (`parsePath` produces string segments, integer-like segments serialize to `"0"`/`"3"`, etc.) and exercised the live CLI behavior shown above.
- Happy to address any review findings on the PR.

Closes #80722.
target.author: kiranmagic7
